title: Unit 02 Pretest
description: Diagnostic pretest for Supply, Demand, and Market Structures.

questions.prompt: According to the law of demand, what happens when price increases?

choices.feedback: This is incorrect. When price increases, consumers usually buy less, not more.
Correct. The law of demand states that as price increases, quantity demanded decreases.
This choice refers to producer behavior, not what happens to quantity demanded.
A price change causes movement along the demand curve, not a shift in demand.

questions.prompt: A change in consumer income can shift the demand curve.

choices.feedback: Correct. Income is one of the factors that can shift demand.
This is incorrect. Changes in income can change consumer buying behavior and shift demand.

questions.prompt: What does the law of supply state?

choices.text: As price increases, quantity supplied increases
choices.feedback: Correct. The law of supply states that higher prices usually lead producers to supply more.
This choice describes demand and is also inaccurate in this context.
This is the opposite of the law of supply.
Price does affect the quantity producers are willing to supply.
This describes a surplus, not equilibrium.
This describes a shortage, not equilibrium.

choices.text: When quantity demanded equals quantity supplied
choices.feedback: Correct. Equilibrium is the point where quantity demanded and quantity supplied are equal.
Equilibrium is based on balance between buyers and sellers, not simply fixed prices.

questions.prompt: A shortage occurs when quantity demanded is greater than quantity supplied.

choices.feedback: Correct. A shortage exists when buyers want more than sellers are offering at the current price.
This is incorrect. That statement is the definition of a shortage.

questions.prompt: Which situation represents a shift in supply?

choices.text: Price increases and producers supply more
choices.feedback: This is a movement along the supply curve caused by a price change.

choices.text: Consumers buy less due to higher prices
choices.feedback: This describes demand behavior, not a shift in supply.
Correct. New technology can shift supply by changing production conditions.
This is also movement along the supply curve caused by a price change.

questions.prompt: Which market structure has only one seller?

choices.feedback: Perfect competition has many sellers, not one.
Monopolistic competition has many sellers offering similar but different products.
An oligopoly has a few large sellers, not just one.
Correct. A monopoly is a market structure with only one seller.

questions.prompt: In perfect competition, businesses have strong control over price.

choices.feedback: This is incorrect. In perfect competition, no single business has strong control over price.
Correct. In perfect competition, prices are determined by the market rather than by one seller.
